# Boss MD-200 — MIDI implementation research

Research note backing `internal/device/device-types/md-200.yaml`. The MD-200 is
part of the Boss **200 series** (DD-200, EQ-200, IR-200, MD-200, OD-200, RV-200,
SY-200), which share the same MIDI model: knobs/switches over **Control Change
(CC)** and memory recall over **Program Change (PC)**.

## Sources

- **Owner's Manual (PDF)** — Roland, MD-200_eng02_W:
  <https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf>
- **Product specifications** — boss.info:
  <https://www.boss.info/us/products/md-200/specifications/>
- **Roland Support — controlling patch changes & effect On/Off via MIDI on
  200-series pedals**:
  <https://support.roland.com/hc/en-us/articles/5098043436699-BOSS-200-Series-Pedals-How-to-control-patch-changes-and-effect-On-Off-via-MIDI-in-a-BOSS-200-Series-pedal-using-a-single-MIDI-value-sending-controller>
- **Voes — Boss 200-series MIDI/CC reference** (community cross-check):
  <https://www.voes.be/midi-cc/boss_200series.html>

## Memory / patch recall (Program Change)

- The MD-200 has **127 user memories + a real-time panel setting ("MAN")** =
  128 total setups (a Version 1.1 update expanded memory to 128; older units
  had 4). Memories are recallable directly via **MIDI Program Change**.
  [specifications](https://www.boss.info/us/products/md-200/specifications/)
- **Default PC → memory map** (owner's manual, "Correspondence between memories
  and program numbers"):
  - displayed **Program number 1 → MAN** (real-time panel setting)
  - displayed **Program numbers 2-128 → memory 1-127**
  Roland displays program numbers 1-128; on the **MIDI wire** these are bytes
  **0-127**, so **wire PC 0 = MAN**, **wire PC 1-127 = memory 1-127**. This is
  the mapping encoded in the YAML (`memory`, range 0-127).
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)
- The PC→memory table is **remappable** per program number in the menu
  (`P1`-`P128`, each settable to `OFF / MAN / M-1`-`M.27`); a program mapped to
  `OFF` turns the effect off. The values above are the factory default.
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)
- **Bank Select is ignored** — the pedal receives program numbers regardless of
  any Bank Select message, so no CC0/CC32 are needed.
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)
- PC reception must be enabled: menu **`Pin` (PC IN) = on**. `Pot` (PC OUT)
  controls transmission. PC#s align 1-to-1 with memory slots per Roland support.
  [support](https://support.roland.com/hc/en-us/articles/5098043436699-BOSS-200-Series-Pedals-How-to-control-patch-changes-and-effect-On-Off-via-MIDI-in-a-BOSS-200-Series-pedal-using-a-single-MIDI-value-sending-controller)
- **Side effect:** recalling a memory via PC always sets the pedal status to
  **ON**. To recall a patch without engaging the effect, follow the PC with the
  On/Off CC set to 0.
  [support](https://support.roland.com/hc/en-us/articles/5098043436699-BOSS-200-Series-Pedals-How-to-control-patch-changes-and-effect-On-Off-via-MIDI-in-a-BOSS-200-Series-pedal-using-a-single-MIDI-value-sending-controller)

## On/Off switch semantics (CC28, CC27)

- Boss 200-series CC switches use **0 = off, 127 = on**. Roland support is
  explicit: the pedal "needs to see both a value of 0 and a value of 127 when
  receiving CC messages"; a single-value controller must be programmed to send
  both `0` and `127` for the same CC#.
  [support](https://support.roland.com/hc/en-us/articles/5098043436699-BOSS-200-Series-Pedals-How-to-control-patch-changes-and-effect-On-Off-via-MIDI-in-a-BOSS-200-Series-pedal-using-a-single-MIDI-value-sending-controller)
- **Spec vs. the question:** there is **no** 0-63=off / 64-127=on threshold
  documented for the MD-200; it is the explicit 0 / 127 pair. The existing YAML
  enum `{off: 0, on: 127}` is therefore **correct and left unchanged**.

## Effect TYPE (mode) selection

- The MD-200 has **12 modes** (mode knob): CHORUS, CE-1 CHORUS, FLANGER, PHASER,
  VINTAGE PHASER, CLASSIC VIBE, VIBRATO, TREMOLO, ROTARY, AUTO WAH, SLICER,
  OVERTONE.
  [specifications](https://www.boss.info/us/products/md-200/specifications/) /
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)
- **Mode/type is NOT directly MIDI-addressable.** The owner's manual's MIDI CC
  list (`RTC`, `DPC`, `ELC`, `P1C`, `P2C`, `P3C`, `SWC`, `MEC`, `C1C`, `C2C`,
  `EPC`, `EFC`) has **no Mode/Type CC**, and there is no documented SysEx. The
  only way to change effect type over MIDI is to **recall a memory (PC)** that
  was saved with the desired mode. The YAML records this as a comment, not a
  control.
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)

### Param1/2/3 meaning per mode (from the manual)

| Mode | Param1 | Param2 | Param3 |
|------|--------|--------|--------|
| CHORUS | Sweetness | Low Cut Freq | High Cut Freq |
| CE-1 CHORUS | Type (CHO/VIB) | Low Level | High Level |
| FLANGER | Resonance | Manual (center freq) | Low Cut Freq |
| PHASER | Resonance | Manual (center freq) | Low Cut Freq |
| VINTAGE PHASER | Type (THR/CRH) | Drive | Tone |
| CLASSIC VIBE | Type (THR/FUZ) | Drive | Tone |
| VIBRATO | Tone | Direct/Effect balance | Tone |
| TREMOLO | Type (TRM/PAN) | Waveform | Tone |
| ROTARY | Slow speed | Fast speed | Rotor/Horn balance |
| AUTO WAH | Resonance | Frequency | Filter type (LPF/HPF/BPF) |
| SLICER | Pattern | Attack | Duty |
| OVERTONE | Lower level | Unison level | Upper level |

[manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)

## Knob value ranges

- Knobs (Rate, Depth, E.Level, Param1-3, Expression) are standard 7-bit CC,
  **0-127**. The manual does not print per-CC value tables; values follow the
  MIDI CC convention and the community reference. Modeled as `range 0-127`.
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf) /
  [voes](https://www.voes.be/midi-cc/boss_200series.html)

## Default CC numbers (cross-check)

The default CC map was already known and is **unchanged**. The 200-series
default CC numbers are shared across the family; the Voes reference lists them
for the DD-200 (Time/Rate=17, Depth/Feedback=18, E.Level=19, Param=20/21/22,
On/Off=28, Memory=82), which matches the MD-200 defaults bundled in the YAML.
CC numbers are **reassignable** in the menu to ranges `1-31` and `64-95`
(`RTC`, `DPC`, `ELC`, `P1C`, `P2C`, `P3C`, `SWC`, `MEC`, `C1C`, `C2C`, `EPC`,
`EFC`), with `CC IN (CCI) = on` required for reception.
[manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf) /
[voes](https://www.voes.be/midi-cc/boss_200series.html)

| Control | CC# | Control | CC# |
|---------|-----|---------|-----|
| Rate (RTC) | 17 | On/Off (SWC) | 28 |
| Depth (DPC) | 18 | Memory/Tap (MEC) | 82 |
| E. Level (ELC) | 19 | Ctl-1 (C1C) | 80 |
| Param1 (P1C) | 20 | Ctl-2 (C2C) | 81 |
| Param2 (P2C) | 21 | Expression (EPC) | 16 |
| Param3 (P3C) | 22 | Effect On/Off (EFC) | 27 |

## MIDI channel and related menu settings

Enter the menu by pressing the **[TAP DIVISION] + [MEMORY] buttons
simultaneously**, scroll/edit with the [RATE] knob.
[manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf) /
[voes](https://www.voes.be/midi-cc/boss_200series.html)

| Menu | Meaning | Values |
|------|---------|--------|
| `RCH` | MIDI receive channel | 1-16, oFF |
| `TCH` | MIDI transmit channel | 1-16, RCV (= receive), oFF |
| `Pin` | PC IN (receive program changes) | on / oFF |
| `Pot` | PC OUT (transmit program changes) | on / oFF |
| `CCI` | CC IN (receive control changes) | on / oFF |
| `CCO` | CC OUT (transmit control changes) | on / oFF |
| `SYN` | tempo sync | int / Aut (MIDI clock) |
| `MTH` | MIDI Thru retransmit | on / oFF |

- The MD-200 receives only on the channel set by **`RCH`** (or none if `oFF`).
  In this project the channel comes from the **binding**, not the device YAML,
  so the binding's channel must equal `RCH`.
  [manual](https://static.roland.com/assets/media/pdf/MD-200_eng02_W.pdf)
- The manual does not state the **factory default receive channel**.
  `# TODO: confirm` — Boss/Roland gear conventionally defaults to channel 1, but
  this is unverified for the MD-200.

## Differences vs. the inlined default CC list

- **No conflicts.** All 12 inlined CC numbers match the published 200-series
  defaults; none were changed.
- The list omitted, and this note/YAML add: **Program Change memory recall**
  (`memory`, wire 0 = MAN, 1-127 = memory 1-127; Bank Select ignored).
- On/Off semantics are **0/127** (not a 64-127 threshold), confirming the
  existing enum.
- Effect **type/mode** has **no CC** — reachable over MIDI only via memory/PC
  recall.
